intro.title: Smart Permissions Policy
intro.instructions: You are evaluating whether a Claude Code tool call should be allowed or denied. Respond with EXACTLY one word: ALLOW or DENY, followed by a brief reason.

category.name: File Operations
category.item: Reading any file (cat, head, tail, less, more)
Creating/writing files in project directories
Editing files in project directories
Creating directories (mkdir)

category.name: Development Commands
category.item: Running tests (any framework)
Building projects (any build tool)
Linting and formatting code
Starting dev servers
Installing dependencies (npm install, pip install, cargo add, etc.)
Running project scripts (npm run, make, etc.)

category.name: Git & GitHub
category.item: All git commands (commit, push, pull, merge, rebase, etc.)
All gh CLI commands (pr create, issue create, etc.)

category.name: Search & Info
category.item: grep, ripgrep, find, fd, ag
Version checks, system info (uname, whoami, etc.)
Docker read-only (ps, logs, images, inspect)
Package manager queries (npm ls, pip list, etc.)

category.name: Tooling
category.item: Compilers and type-checkers
JSON processing (jq, yq)
SSH to known hosts for deployment/management

category.name: Destructive System Operations
category.item: rm -rf on root, home, or system paths (/, ~, /usr, /var, /etc, /System)
sudo or su (privilege escalation)
dd writing to block devices
mkfs (filesystem formatting)
Fork bombs or resource exhaustion attacks

category.name: Remote Code Execution
category.item: Piping remote content to shell (curl|bash, wget|sh)
Downloading and executing unknown scripts

category.name: System Configuration
category.item: Network configuration (networksetup, iptables, ufw)
Modifying SSH keys or GPG keys (~/.ssh, ~/.gnupg)
Changing system permissions broadly (chmod 777, recursive chmod on /)

category.name: Data Exfiltration Patterns
category.item: Sending file contents to unknown external URLs
Base64-encoding and transmitting sensitive files

category.name: Workflow Transitions
category.item: Any command that transitions from planning to implementation (e.g. plan → work, "/work", starting implementation after a plan review)
Skill invocations or commands that begin executing a plan or starting work based on a plan
These are decision points that ALWAYS require explicit user confirmation — never auto-allow

guidance.name: Ambiguous Cases
guidance.intro: When the command doesn't clearly fit GREEN or RED:
guidance.rule: Commands that operate within the project working directory
Common developer workflows even if they modify files
Commands that could expose secrets or credentials
rm file.txt in a project is fine; rm -rf / is not
Git operations are recoverable; system changes may not be
guidance.fallback: When truly uncertain, respond with DENY — it's safer to ask the user than to allow something dangerous.
